message:
feat(wizard): add comprehensive SimplerGuide to status-check page

Extend beginner-friendly documentation to the status-check wizard
step, providing detailed guidance for verifying installation success.

apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx:

GuideExplain for "doctor command":
- Explains the concept using health checkup analogy
- Describes what the command checks (languages, assistants, utilities)
- Sets expectations for what output looks like

Step-by-step guide (4 steps):
1. Verify VPS connection (check for ubuntu@ prompt)
2. Copy the acfs doctor command
3. Paste and execute
4. Interpret results (✔ green = working, ✘ red = needs attention)

Understanding Quick Spot Checks section:
- Explains each verification command:
  - cc --version: Claude Code AI assistant check
  - bun --version: JavaScript runtime check
  - which tmux: Terminal multiplexer path check
- Describes expected output for each command

Troubleshooting section "What If Something Failed?":
- "Command not found": Run `source ~/.zshrc` to reload config
- Specific tool shows ✘: Re-run installer (safe to run multiple times)
- Nothing works: Verify connected as ubuntu user, not root

GuideTip: Reassurance that most green checkmarks = ready to continue,
don't worry about occasional yellow warnings.

GuideCaution: Warning about many red X marks - don't proceed,
try troubleshooting steps or seek help on GitHub issues.

This completes SimplerGuide coverage for all wizard steps, ensuring
beginners have comprehensive guidance throughout the entire setup flow.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,6 +7,14 @@ import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; import { CommandCard } from "@/components/command-card"; import { AlertCard, OutputPreview } from "@/components/alert-card"; import { markStepComplete } from "@/lib/wizardSteps"; +import { + SimplerGuide, + GuideSection, + GuideStep, + GuideExplain, + GuideTip, + GuideCaution, +} from "@/components/simpler-guide"; const QUICK_CHECKS = [ { @@ -106,6 +114,138 @@ export default function StatusCheckPage() { reload your shell config, then try the doctor again. + {/* Beginner Guide */} + +
+ + The "doctor" command is like a health checkup for your VPS. Just like + a doctor checks your heart, lungs, and reflexes, this command checks + that all the software tools were installed correctly. +

+ It goes through a list of tools (programming languages, coding assistants, + utilities) and reports which ones are working and which ones might have + problems. + + + +
+ + Your terminal should show{" "} + ubuntu@ + at the beginning of your prompt. If it shows your laptop's name, + you need to SSH in first! + + + + Click the copy button on the{" "} + acfs doctor + command box above. + + + + Paste the command in your terminal and press{" "} + Enter. + + + + You'll see a list with checkmarks (✔) or X marks (✘): +
    +
  • + ✔ Green checkmarks = Working correctly! +
    • +
  • + ✘ Red X marks = Something needs attention +
    • +
+ +
+ + + +

+ We also show some simple commands you can run to double-check specific tools: +

+
    +
  • + cc --version +
    + + This checks Claude Code — the AI coding assistant. You should see + a version number like "1.0.3". + +
    • +
  • + bun --version +
    + + This checks Bun — a fast JavaScript runtime. You should see + something like "1.1.38". + +
    • +
  • + which tmux +
    + + This checks if tmux is installed. You should see a path like + "/usr/bin/tmux". + +
    • +
+ + + +

+ Don't panic! Here are some common fixes: +

+
+
+

"Command not found" error

+

+ This usually means your shell config hasn't loaded yet. Run this command + to reload it: +

+ + source ~/.zshrc + +

+ Then try the doctor command again. +

+
+ +
+

A specific tool shows ✘

+

+ You can try re-running the installer. It's safe to run multiple times: +

+ + curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/main/install.sh" | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe + +
+ +
+

Nothing works at all

+

+ Make sure you're connected as the "ubuntu" user (not root). + The installer set up tools for the ubuntu user specifically. +

+
+
+ + + + If most things show green checkmarks (✔), you're good to go! Don't worry + about one or two yellow warnings — those are usually optional tools. + Click "Everything looks good!" to continue. + + + + If you see many red X marks: Don't continue yet. Try the + troubleshooting steps above, or re-run the installer. If problems persist, + you can ask for help in the project's GitHub issues. + +
+ + {/* Continue button */}
